title: Perspectives on Cognitive Informatics and Cognitive Computing: Summary of the Panel of IEEE ICCI'09
abstract: Cognitive Informatics (CI) is a transdisciplinary enquiry of computer science, information sciences, cognitive science, and intelligence science that investigates into the internal information processing mechanisms and processes of the brain and natural intelligence, as well as their engineering applications in cognitive computing. This paper summarizes the presentations of a set of 14 position papers in the ICCI'09 Panel on Cognitive Informatics and Cognitive Computing contributed from invited panelists who are part of the world's renowned researchers and scholars in the field of cognitive informatics and cognitive computing.
